output.summary: Update Hero block to fix reported bugs (chevron icon size, texture overlay), update test page to new DemoContainer standards with comprehensive variation coverage, and verify accessibility compliance against block type requirements.

accessibilityReview.currentImplementation: The Hero block currently has: (1) <section> element with aria-labelledby automatically added by theme_block_props when heading exists, linking to the heading's ID via theme_block_region_id(). (2) <h1> heading tag for page-level heading hierarchy. (3) Background image rendered via wp_get_attachment_image with alt text from media library. (4) QuickNavigation select has aria-label matching the title text. (5) SVG chevron icon has aria-hidden='true'. (6) Submit button uses semantic <button> element with visible text.
accessibilityReview.requirements: Block type documentation (hero.md) requires: (1) Proper heading hierarchy, typically <h1> for page hero - SATISFIED. (2) Follows Landmarks pattern (accessibility-patterns/landmarks.md) for semantic structure - SATISFIED via <section> with aria-labelledby. (3) Background images have appropriate alt text or are decorative (alt='') - PARTIALLY MET, images use media library alt text which may or may not be appropriate. (4) Text over images meets WCAG AA contrast ratio - SATISFIED via gradient overlays. (5) General accessibility requirements (patterns/accessibility.md): focus visible on interactive elements, keyboard accessible, semantic HTML - MOSTLY SATISFIED.
gaps: QuickNavigation select element uses aria-label from the title attribute, but if title is empty, the select has no accessible name. Should add a fallback.

Background images in the hero are decorative (they sit behind text with gradient overlays), but they use the media library alt text instead of alt=''. Per accessibility guidelines, decorative images should have alt=''.

QuickNavigation submit button navigates to a URL (window.location.href) but uses <button> instead of <a>. Since it performs navigation, this is semantically a link. However, given the form-like interaction pattern (select + submit), <button> is acceptable. No change needed.

No visible <label> element for the QuickNavigation select - uses aria-label only. This is acceptable for the visual design but could be improved. The title text serves as a visual label, so associating it properly would be ideal.
QuickNavigation dropdown options with black background may have contrast issues in some browsers' native select rendering. Low risk since browsers handle native select styling.
recommendations: Add alt='' to background images in Hero.php since they are purely decorative (behind text with gradient overlays). Change the wp_get_attachment_image call to force empty alt.
Add a fallback aria-label on the QuickNavigation select when title is empty, e.g., aria-label='Navigation' or similar.
Consider adding a visually-hidden <label> element for the select instead of relying solely on aria-label, but this is minor and current implementation is acceptable.

testPageReview.currentStructure: The test page (id: 75, slug: test-hero-block, title: 'TEST: Hero Block') currently has 2 block instances: (1) Primary variation with image, quick navigation with 3 links and random dropdown behavior. (2) Secondary variation with image, one CTA button. Separated by a core/heading block as divider. Does NOT use DemoContainer wrappers. Title follows old pattern 'TEST: Hero Block' instead of new 'TEST: Hero'.
testPageReview.standardRequirements: New test page standards require: (1) Every variation wrapped in takt/demo-container with descriptive title and description. (2) Page title follows 'TEST: {BlockName}' pattern (no 'Block' suffix). (3) Required variations: Default/Minimal, Full Content, Long Content edge case, Empty Optional Fields, each attribute option, each block variation. (4) For Hero specifically: both primary and secondary variations, quick navigation toggle states, dropdown behavior options (first/placeholder/random), with/without image, long content, many links, empty links.
changes: Update page title from 'TEST: Hero Block' to 'TEST: Hero'
Wrap all block instances in DemoContainer blocks with descriptive titles and descriptions
Remove core/heading divider blocks (DemoContainer provides section separation)

Add missing variations: primary minimal, no image, secondary minimal, secondary multiple buttons, placeholder dropdown behavior, first-item dropdown behavior, long content edge case, empty optional fields, quick nav with no links, quick nav with many links (10+)

bugFixes.bug: Chevron icon size - 20px instead of Figma's 24px
bugFixes.approach: Update IconChevron.svg viewBox and dimensions from 20x20 to 24x24. Scale the path coordinates proportionally: multiply all path values by 1.2 (24/20). Current path: 'M5 7.5L10 12.5L15 7.5' becomes 'M6 9L12 15L18 9'. Update width/height attributes to 24.

bugFixes.bug: Texture overlay missing on QuickNavigation background
bugFixes.approach: Add a grain/noise texture overlay div inside the QuickNavigation container. Requires: (1) A noise texture image asset (SVG or small PNG). (2) An overlay div with mix-blend-mode: color-dodge, opacity: 8% (0.08), positioned absolutely over the bg-charcoal area. (3) Add this in both QuickNavigation.php and QuickNavigation.tsx for sync. NOTE: This requires a texture asset - either create an SVG noise pattern or source a grain texture image. The Developer Agent should create an inline SVG noise pattern using CSS (e.g., using a repeating background with a tiny noise tile or CSS filter) to avoid an external asset dependency.

fileChanges.description: Update chevron icon from 20px to 24px per Figma spec
fileChanges.details: Change width='20' height='20' viewBox='0 0 20 20' to width='24' height='24' viewBox='0 0 24 24'. Update path d attribute from 'M5 7.5L10 12.5L15 7.5' to 'M6 9L12 15L18 9' (scaled 1.2x).

fileChanges.description: Add texture overlay for grain effect on dark background, add aria-label fallback for empty title
fileChanges.details: Add a div with grain texture overlay using mix-blend-mode: color-dodge and opacity 8% inside the quick-navigation container. Add position: relative to container (already has it implicitly via flex). Add fallback aria-label when title is empty.

fileChanges.description: Add texture overlay div in editor preview to match PHP output, sync with PHP changes
fileChanges.details: Add the same grain texture overlay div in the editor component. This is lower priority since it's editor-only visual fidelity.

fileChanges.description: Force decorative alt='' on background images
fileChanges.details: Change the wp_get_attachment_image call to include 'alt' => '' since the hero background image is purely decorative (behind text with gradient overlays). The current implementation uses the media library's alt text which may contain descriptive text, but since the image is decorative in this context, it should be empty.

fileChanges.description: No TSX changes needed for alt text since ImageDropUploader handles it differently in editor. Minor: ensure parity with PHP.
fileChanges.details: The TSX uses ImageDropUploader which manages its own alt text. No change needed for the editor experience.

designDecisions.question: What noise/grain texture approach to use for the QuickNavigation overlay?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use a CSS-only approach with a small inline SVG data URI noise pattern as background-image on a pseudo-element or overlay div. This avoids adding an external asset file. A common pattern is a 200x200 noise tile repeated across the surface. Example: background-image: url("data:image/svg+xml,...") with a <filter> using feTurbulence for noise generation.

designDecisions.question: Should the background image alt text be forced to empty string or use the media library alt?
designDecisions.recommendation: Force alt='' since the image is decorative in the hero context (behind gradient overlays and text). Screen readers should skip it. This follows WCAG guidance for decorative images.

variations.demoDescription: Primary variation with all ThemeHeading fields populated (eyebrow, heading, description). Background image with focal point. Quick navigation enabled with multiple links, random dropdown behavior.
Only heading populated, no eyebrow, no description. Quick navigation disabled. Tests shouldDisplay behavior for empty optional fields.
Primary variation without background image. Tests rendering without media.
Secondary variation with left-aligned content, all fields populated including CTA button.
Secondary variation with only heading. No eyebrow, no description, no buttons.
Secondary variation with primary and secondary button variations.
Tests placeholder dropdown behavior with disabled placeholder option.

variations.demoTitle: Primary Variation - Dropdown Behavior: First Item

variations.demoDescription: Very long heading (100+ chars), long eyebrow, long description, long dropdown labels.
Only heading populated, no image, no other fields. Tests graceful degradation.
Quick navigation enabled but no links added. Tests empty dropdown state.
Dropdown with 10 options. Tests select element with many items.

interactiveTests.expect: Native select dropdown opens showing link options

interactiveTests.name: QuickNavigation button click triggers navigation
interactiveTests.expect: Submit button is focusable and shows visible focus indicator
